body.title: Semester Examination - Design and Analysis of Algorithms

questions.question: Define Big-O, Big-Omega, and Big-Theta notations with suitable examples.
Differentiate between Dynamic Programming and Greedy approach.
List four applications of Depth-First Search (DFS) algorithm.
Explain the concept of Amortized Analysis with a suitable example.
What are NP-Complete problems? Give an example.

sections.instructions: Attempt all questions. Each question carries 4 marks.

questions.question: Solve the following recurrence relations using the Master Theorem: (i) T(n) = 3T(n/4) + n log n (ii) T(n) = 2T(n/2) + n
Explain Dijkstra's single-source shortest path algorithm. Trace its execution on a graph of your choice.
Describe Kruskal's algorithm for finding the Minimum Spanning Tree. Apply it to a given weighted undirected graph with at least 5 vertices.
Write the algorithm for finding the Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) of two strings using Dynamic Programming. Illustrate with an example.

internalChoice.question: Explain the 0/1 Knapsack problem and formulate its solution using Dynamic Programming.

questions.question: Explain the N-Queens problem and outline a backtracking algorithm to solve it. Illustrate with N=4.

internalChoice.question: Describe the Subset Sum problem and discuss how backtracking can be used to find a solution.

sections.instructions: Attempt any five questions. Each question carries 10 marks. Some questions have internal choices.

questions.question: Explain the Floyd-Warshall algorithm for all-pairs shortest path problem. Illustrate its working with an example graph containing at least 4 vertices.

internalChoice.question: Describe the Bellman-Ford algorithm to find shortest paths from a single source in a graph with negative edge weights. Explain its limitations and trace it on an example graph.

questions.question: Explain the Ford-Fulkerson algorithm for finding the maximum flow in a flow network. Illustrate with an example.

internalChoice.question: State and prove the Max-Flow Min-Cut Theorem. Discuss its significance in network flow problems.

sections.instructions: Attempt any two questions. Each question carries 15 marks. Each question has an internal choice.

body.subject: Design and Analysis of Algorithms
